mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 285 in CPIAUCSL signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 285.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS (labor demand) and declining UNRATE (labor supply) signals tight labor markets, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows Rank IC < 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO (industrial production) indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which drives corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO changes (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth (GDPC1) in the prior quarter predicts sustained consumer demand, driving S&P 500 returns higher over 60 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining UNRATE (unemployment) signals tighter labor markets, increasing wage pressures that boost corporate revenues and generate positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with UNRATE changes (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS (consumer spending) indicates strong household demand, which drives corporate sales and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with RSAFS changes (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS above 2% signals monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2%.
